question: Why does the CMS note store content definitions as JSON schema/config objects?

options.text: To let tenants add content types without running database migrations.
options.feedback: Correct: schema flexibility moves model evolution into tenant-level configuration.

options.text: To guarantee strict compile-time typing directly inside PostgreSQL rows.
options.feedback: JSONB is flexible runtime data, not compile-time static typing enforcement.

options.text: To remove the need for relational links between entries and definitions.
options.feedback: Entries still reference definitions through identifiers to maintain structure mapping.

options.text: To force every tenant to share one immutable global content model.
options.feedback: The design exists to support tenant-specific models, not one rigid schema.

whyQuestion: How do `content_definitions` and `content_entries` work together in this design?

whyOptions.text: Definitions store field blueprint; entries store item data referencing that blueprint.
whyOptions.feedback: Exactly: one defines structure, the other stores actual records and values.

whyOptions.text: Definitions store published posts, while entries only store tenant membership roles.
whyOptions.feedback: Membership is separate; entries hold content items tied to definitions.

whyOptions.text: Definitions and entries are duplicates kept for backup and rollback only.
whyOptions.feedback: They represent distinct responsibilities, not redundant backup copies.

whyOptions.text: Definitions are static SQL views, while entries are temporary cache rows.
whyOptions.feedback: The note describes JSON-backed domain entities, not view-cache architecture.
